title: Standard and Extended ACL Configuration
description: Configure standard and extended named ACLs to filter traffic, apply them to interfaces, and verify their operation.
instructions:
## Standard and Extended ACL Configuration Lab

Configure ACLs to enforce the following security policies on router R1.

### Topology

```
[Sales LAN]           [R1]           [Server LAN]
10.1.10.0/24 --- Gi0/0      Gi0/1 --- 10.1.50.0/24
                                      Web Server: 10.1.50.10
                                      SSH Server: 10.1.50.20
[Engineering LAN]
10.1.20.0/24 --- Gi0/2
```

### Security Policy

#### Part 1: Extended Named ACL
Create an extended named ACL called `SALES-POLICY` and apply it inbound on Gi0/0:
1. **Permit** Sales hosts to access the web server (10.1.50.10) on HTTP (80) and HTTPS (443)
2. **Deny** Sales hosts from accessing the SSH server (10.1.50.20) on port 22
3. **Permit** all ICMP traffic from Sales to the Server LAN (for ping troubleshooting)
4. **Deny** all other traffic from Sales to the Server LAN (implicit deny handles this, but add an explicit deny with logging)
5. **Permit** all other traffic (so Sales can still reach Engineering and the Internet)

#### Part 2: Standard Named ACL for VTY Access
Create a standard named ACL called `VTY-ACCESS`:
6. **Permit** only the network admin host (10.1.20.5) to SSH into R1
7. Apply it to VTY lines 0-4 using `access-class`
starterCode:
enable
configure terminal
!
! Part 1: Extended ACL for Sales traffic
!
!
! Apply to interface
!
!
! Part 2: Standard ACL for VTY access
!
!
! Apply to VTY lines
!
!
end
solutionCode:
enable
configure terminal
!
! Part 1: Extended named ACL
ip access-list extended SALES-POLICY
 permit tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.10 eq 80
 permit tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.10 eq 443
 deny tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.20 eq 22
 permit icmp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 10.1.50.0 0.0.0.255
 deny ip 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 10.1.50.0 0.0.0.255 log
 permit ip any any
 exit
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 ip access-group SALES-POLICY in
 exit
!
! Part 2: Standard ACL for VTY
ip access-list standard VTY-ACCESS
 permit host 10.1.20.5
 exit
!
line vty 0 4
 access-class VTY-ACCESS in
 exit
!
end
expectedOutput:
R1#show access-lists
Extended IP access list SALES-POLICY
    10 permit tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.10 eq www (45 matches)
    20 permit tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.10 eq 443 (32 matches)
    30 deny tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.20 eq 22 (5 matches)
    40 permit icmp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 10.1.50.0 0.0.0.255 (12 matches)
    50 deny ip 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 10.1.50.0 0.0.0.255 log (3 matches)
    60 permit ip any any (200 matches)
Standard IP access list VTY-ACCESS
    10 permit 10.1.20.5 (2 matches)

R1#show ip interface GigabitEthernet0/0
GigabitEthernet0/0 is up, line protocol is up
  Internet address is 10.1.10.1/24
  Broadcast address is 255.255.255.255
  Inbound  access list is SALES-POLICY
  Outgoing access list is not set

R1#show ip interface brief
Interface              IP-Address      OK? Method Status Protocol
Gi0/0                  10.1.10.1       YES manual up     up
Gi0/1                  10.1.50.1       YES manual up     up
Gi0/2                  10.1.20.1       YES manual up     up

R1#show line vty 0 4
   Tty Line Typ     Tx/Rx    A Modem  Roty AccO AccI  Uses  Noise  Overruns   Int
    98   98 VTY              -    -      -    -    -     0      0     0/0       -
    99   99 VTY              -    -      -    -    -     0      0     0/0       -
   100  100 VTY              -    -      -    -    -     0      0     0/0       -
   101  101 VTY              -    -      -    -    -     0      0     0/0       -
   102  102 VTY              -    -      -    -    -     0      0     0/0       -

  Line(s) have access class VTY-ACCESS set for incoming connections.

R1#show running-config
Building configuration...

Current configuration:
!
hostname R1
!
ip access-list extended SALES-POLICY
 permit tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.10 eq www
 permit tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.10 eq 443
 deny tcp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 host 10.1.50.20 eq 22
 permit icmp 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 10.1.50.0 0.0.0.255
 deny ip 10.1.10.0 0.0.0.255 10.1.50.0 0.0.0.255 log
 permit ip any any
!
ip access-list standard VTY-ACCESS
 permit host 10.1.20.5
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/0
 ip address 10.1.10.1 255.255.255.0
 ip access-group SALES-POLICY in
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 ip address 10.1.50.1 255.255.255.0
 no shutdown
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/2
 ip address 10.1.20.1 255.255.255.0
 no shutdown
!
line vty 0 4
 access-class VTY-ACCESS in
!
end
hints: ACL order matters! Place more specific permit rules before broader deny rules. The first match wins and stops processing. If you deny SSH before permitting HTTP, the HTTP permit won't be reached for the SSH server.
Use 'host 10.1.50.10' as a shortcut for '10.1.50.10 0.0.0.0' (matches a single host). Use wildcard 0.0.0.255 to match an entire /24 subnet.
Extended ACLs should be applied close to the source (inbound on Gi0/0). Standard ACLs should be applied close to the destination. For VTY lines, use 'access-class' instead of 'ip access-group'.
learningObjectives: Configure extended named ACLs with protocol and port-specific filtering
Understand ACL processing order and the importance of rule sequencing
Apply extended ACLs to interfaces in the correct direction
Configure standard ACLs to restrict VTY management access
